title: Here, ever since you went abroad,
content:
Here, ever since you went abroad,
    If there be change, no change I see,
    I only walk our wonted road,
    The road is only walkt by me.

    Yes; I forgot; a change there is;
    Was it of _that_ you bade me tell?
    I catch at times, at times I miss
    The sight, the tone, I know so well.

    Only two months since you stood here!
    Two shortest months! then tell me why
    Voices are harsher than they were,
    And tears are longer ere they dry.
meaning:
Walter Savage Landor's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Here, ever since you went abroad,"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
